purpose: Keep operational, maintenance and future GitHub publishing documentation separate from knowledge content and system behavior truth.
principle: Technical documentation is an operations surface. It explains how to set up, validate, maintain and publish the repository, while machine-readable registries, schemas and scripts remain the system truth.

machine_truth.role: Canonical map for technical documentation boundaries
Module placement and documentation boundary truth
Git governance and remote-action boundary truth

doc_routes.boundary: Only explains where technical documentation starts and which file owns which topic.
Explains how to open, inspect and prepare the repository locally.

doc_routes.role: Routine maintenance commands and operational loop
doc_routes.boundary: Explains regular maintenance and validation habits.

doc_routes.role: Validation command map and when to run each command
doc_routes.boundary: Explains validation workflow and command order for maintainers.

doc_routes.role: Future GitHub publication checklist and repository hygiene
doc_routes.boundary: Explains pre-publication checks, sensitive-data review and external-reader readiness.

doc_routes.role: Common local maintenance failures and diagnostic routing
doc_routes.boundary: Explains common failure symptoms and which diagnostic route to use.

split_policy.baseline_doc_rule: docs/technical/README.md stays thin. Detailed operations, validation, GitHub readiness and troubleshooting each live in their own routed file.
split_policy.machine_truth_rule: Technical docs explain how to operate the repository; JSON registries, schemas and scripts decide behavior.
split_policy.github_rule: GitHub publication documentation can prepare local checklists and drafts, but actual remote publication requires explicit user approval.
split_policy.growth_trigger: When a new operational topic becomes durable, add a docs/technical/*.md file and register it in doc_routes before relying on it.
